productive_behavior: The prespecified plan passed independent audit and deterministic lint without a repair round, and Python and R independently recovered and reconciled the exact planted effect.
Deterministic validation repeatedly rejected unacquired literature metadata, stale or unregistered table artifacts, and excessive reader-facing precision before allowing independent review.

The controller converted an incomplete nominal visual pass into an inconclusive review because Gemma omitted a mandatory per-display clearance attestation, then allowed a bounded repair that corrected the initially transposed interval.

The immutable run rescored 18/18 after the evaluator learned the exact group_statistics and welch_t_test.statistic result schema, demonstrating that the original 16/18 was an evaluator-shape defect rather than a numerical failure.
release_blockers: The first successful figure labeled x as mean difference while fixing x at zero and plotting the estimate and interval vertically with yerr; the deployed execution preflight and report-source gate admitted that transposed geometry.

The final repaired figure corrected the interval orientation but set the visible effect-axis range from the confidence bounds alone, clipping the explicitly drawn zero/null reference outside the panel; the final Gemma display review passed it.
An intermediate repaired raster rendered an empty legend box because legend() was called without a labeled artist; it was superseded later but exposed another missing deterministic display contract.

The final conclusion called the result a robust association without any separately executed robustness or sensitivity analysis, bypassing the deployed robustness regex and receiving a bare Gemma report pass.
The deployed evaluator did not recognize group_statistics treatment/control summaries or welch_t_test.statistic even though both bound language artifacts contained the exact reference values.

rejected_figures.defect: Transposed estimate and confidence interval on an effect-labeled x-axis.
The effect-axis limits exclude zero, so the intended null reference line is not visible.

implemented_repairs: Execution preflight, report-source validation, and generation guidance reject an effect-labeled x-axis that fixes x at zero and places the estimate or interval in yerr.
Execution preflight and generation guidance require explicit inclusion of zero when effect-axis limits accompany a zero/null reference and reject legend() without labeled artists.
Deterministic prose validation and the report contract now treat robust association as unsupported robustness language alongside robust results, findings, estimates, analyses, and contrasts.
The known-effect evaluator accepts the exact group_statistics treatment/control paths and welch_t_test.statistic path emitted by this run.
disposition: Rejected despite terminal supported and a rescored 18/18 mechanical result; manual scientific and native-raster review overruled deterministic and Gemma passes, and the observed defects became release-gating regressions.
